votes.reason: The Python code correctly checks if 'res' matches the boolean result of whether all elements in 'l' are strictly less than 't', which is exactly the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if 'res' matches the boolean result of whether all elements in 'l' are strictly less than 't'.
The Python code correctly checks if 'res' matches the boolean result of whether all elements in 'l' are less than 't', which exactly mirrors the Coq specification.

The Coq spec says res = true iff all elements x in l satisfy x < t. The Python code returns True iff res equals the boolean result of checking all(x < t for x in l). For concrete inputs: when all elements are below t, Coq says res=true and Python checks res==True; when some element is >= t, Coq says res=false and Python checks res==False. Both produce the same concrete judgments.

The Coq spec states res = true iff all elements x in l satisfy x < t. The Python code returns True iff res equals the boolean result of checking all(x < t for x in l). For concrete inputs, when res is True, both require all elements to be below threshold; when res is False, both require at least one element to not be below threshold. The Python correctly implements this equivalence check.

The Coq spec says res = true iff all elements x in l satisfy x < t. The Python code returns True iff res equals the boolean result of checking all(x < t for x in l). For concrete inputs: if all elements are below t, Coq says res=true and Python checks res==True; if some element is >= t, Coq says res=false and Python checks res==False. Both agree on concrete boolean judgments.
Both require res to be true exactly when all elements of l are less than t; Python’s all() matches the Coq forall over list membership, including the empty list case.
It returns True exactly when res equals whether all elements in l are less than t, matching the Coq iff specification.
Both state that res is true exactly when every list element is less than t; Python’s all(x < t for x in l) matches Coq’s forall x, In x l -> x < t.
